emoGAN (2021)

collab with @fabiolalariosm where we combined our datasets of scraped emo selfies to train a machine learning model to generate new emos

we will soon mint a video of our results on @withfoundation

#stylegan #machinelearning #machinelearningart
Some scifi cyberpunky fantasy spacey GAN art ive been making to get lost in 💜☄️ made with artbreeder.com 🤷‍♀️

I put object detection, pose estimation, and face detection on top of recent footage in HK and BLM protests and historic footage of black panthers to raise awareness of surveillance and facial recogniton.

Cover your face when you protest!

3d printed a butterfly and integrated an adafruit feather board that receives MQTT twitter messages for the @cybertwee show

Cybertwee Messenger Butterfly

An internet search for ‘cyborg insects’ brings up bizarre and strange images of engineers using insects as biorobotic sensing machines: beetles carrying microcontrollers on their backs, RoboRoaches being remote controlled with electrodes in their antennas as “the world’s first commercially available cyborg.” What is the ethicality behind using animalistic organisms as robots? Is it naive to reimagine a future where insects are used for good, and can they be made into cyborgs without invading their animal rights? The cybertwee messenger butterfly is a sweet & fictional interpretation of how we can think of cyborg insects in a friendlier way. Like a carrier pigeon or a message in a bottle, you can tweet @cyber_butterfly / #cyber_butterfly and the butterfly will virtually receive and deliver your message. Send a peace treaty, a hint to a loved one, or anything else you would like to communicate to the universe.

Commissioned by @bomlab, photos by @thombartley
#netart

a 3d printed kitty I implanted with fiber optic whiskers and an LED for the @cybertwee show

Cybertwee WiFi Kitty

Cats have been historically used as cyborgs experiments, such as the CIA’s Acoustic Kitty project in the 1960’s where cats were implanted with microphones and radio transmitters to be used as spy devices, or as WiFi hacking tools (“WarKitteh” by Gene Bransfield). In a cybertwee world, street cats could have fibre optic whiskers that glow when they detect WiFi. You’ll know if there is an open WiFi network in your area by spotting the neighborhood cat & noticing if their whiskers are pulsing red or flashing rainbow. Their fibre optic whiskers could be programmed to light up by other interactions too. If it is going to rain or if their cat bowl is out of kitty food! It all depends on their owner’s wishes.

Commissioned by @bomlab, photos by @thombartley

my fav sculpture from the @cybertwee
show, by my cute lil collaborate Gabriella Hileman 🐛🍃🌸 Though I Walk Through The Shadow Of The Uncanny Valley (Millipede)

A.I. sex bots, such as those made by Real Doll, are nightmare fuel. This sculpture imagines what would happen if these dolls escaped their gaze-centric creators and took their evolution into their own hands.  Would more practical tasks, such as walking, influence what forms new generations would take?

commissioned by @bomlab, photos by @thombartley
